core_payload.content:
# N-Infinity Runtime Fabric

## 1. Boundary
N-Infinity Runtime Fabric owns the substrate that lets the swarm actually run: the background runtime, orchestration lane, job envelope, continuity state, and night-window posture that make compounding work executable instead of merely conceptual. This sub-hub exists so the swarm's runtime footing stays distinct from the swarm roles, external signal sources, and governance metrics that shape it.

## 2. What Belongs Here
This sub-hub groups `background-agent-runtime`, `symphony`, `capsule-job`, `agent-memory-state`, and `night-shift-autonomy`. Together they define the runtime host, execution workspace lane, bounded work object, continuity memory, and time-window policy that carry N-Infinity from idle topology into real governed iteration.

## 3. What Does Not Belong Here
This sub-hub does not own the role roster of the swarm, the upstream signals that feed it, or the metrics and governance abstractions it uses to reason about quality. Those belong to `swarm-roster`, `signal-fabric`, and `governance-metrics`.

neuro_concentrate.summary: N-Infinity Runtime Fabric groups the execution substrate of the swarm: background runtime, Symphony orchestration, capsule jobs, continuity memory, and night-window posture. It keeps runtime footing separate from role roster, upstream signals, and governance metrics. This matters because compounding work needs a clear home for orchestration, job envelopes, continuity state, and execution posture so the swarm can actually run without hiding its operational substrate inside worker descriptions or signal intake surfaces.

epistemic_ledger.rationale: Minted as part of n-infinity hub decomposition to reduce parent link density from 26 outbound to ≤9.
